Patron of the Hillyard and Him
ois avenue cars are getting a prac-
teal Illustration of the friendly re-
lations which exist between the
Occur d'Alene theater and city au-
thorities. Every evening during the
present week the Hillyard and Him
DIS avenue cars have unloaded and
taken on passengers at Howard
and Front. Every night men,
women and children have had to
take TO the middle of the street
in order to get through the crowd,
much of il comprising dirty. ILL
smelling loafers who congregate in
front of Harry Baer and Dutch
Jake's" vulgarity theater. These
men are attracted from every nook
and corner of the tenderlOin to
watch the pictures thrown on can
vas from the theater. The same
crowd gathers nightly. The police
rigidly enforce the ordinance
against "fakirs' of every descrip-
ton who attract crowds on the
downtown streets. Peddlers are
barred from selling their wares in
the business section of the city.
but the Occur d'Alene theater
maintains greater nuisance night
ly at the very doors of the city
hail without fear of molestation.
Last night the police made no at
tempt to keep a passageway open
for pedestrians, the only officer in
the neighborhood staying at safe
distance. 1t is gradually becom-
ing understood by the people that
they can expect treatment such as
they have had to put up with dur
ing the past week as long as the
Washington Water Power and the
reputed bosses of the tenderloin
are factors In city elections.
